actual_text:
Spice Finch is one of my favorite new restaurants in Philly! I really love this place!! The food is amazing. 

Some of my favorite dishes are: 
1. Date Truffles - wow! Dates with pomegranate & cashew, these little balls are small but packs a delicious mouthful of flavor! Love these. 
2. Stuffed Grape Leaves - these are not your typical grape leaves, like every other Greek restaurant. these are different, with fennel, apricot, and a delicious yogurt sauce. 
3. Lemon Yogurt dipping sauce is absolutely phenomenal. Make sure to get a side of it, its only $4 and worth every penny. Dip in it with the light flaky dough bread, I think its $2.

Some other dishes I really liked was the eggplant meal, carrots app, and cauliflower app. The Octopus I had high hopes for, but it really wasn't as good as I expected. Still decent though. 

The drinks are also amazing. The Clover Club is one of my favorites, but I've also had some others that are great. The Redemption drinks are creative and funny, the midori one was is really good. 

Service is always great, and the ambiance is easy to please anyone.. its fancy enough for a nice date, but casual enough that its not awkward. Its also good for business dinners. The bar is very large so there's usually seats open, but try to make reservations in advance since Spice Finch is so good, that its usually always packed. I love it! Highly recommended.

Although pizza is their game, i am crazy about their sandwiches! In my opinion if a pizza place does sandwiches, give em a try! Why?? Its all in the bread, which is basically the same dough as their pizza (and I love and adore pizza!!).What makes Tampa Pizza so special in particular is everything else in the sandwich is high in quality so i keep forgetting theyre a pizza place. Each sandwich is assembled with love and care every time I order one. Their meats are tender and in just the right proportion, well seasoned and cooked. I love their grilled chicken and hand carved turkey. They also come with quality greens, and sauces. My favorite is the Turkey Bacon swiss sandwich which has a generous portion of that tender turkey I love so much, a pile of flavorful crispy bacon, and swiss cheese melted in their delicious organic bread that is the crown jewel of any sandwich. You can pair the sandwich with fries, but the sandwich itself is so filling i opt for the arugula salad. Its not much to rave about, i mean its salad, but its a good side to go with it if your watching your calories and you want help digesting your food so the meal's not so heavy. Admittedly i have yet to have their pizza, but rest assured I plan on it.

Some of the best Indian food I've had, Ekta Indian Cuisine will satisfy all of your Indian food cravings.

I'm not going to pretend I'm an expert on Indian food, but I can confidently say this is some of the best Indian food on the mainline. Ekta Indian Cuisine is a small regional chain (only three locations) authentic Indian place in Bryn Mawr (parking across the street in a small lot that is usually full from Tin Lizard hipsters). It's causal dining with an upscale look, perfect for a date or a group of friends.

We started with some Garlic Naan ($3.49 - white flour bread flavored with garlic), which is more than enough for two people and was soft, warm, and delicious with the roasted garlic. For our main courses, we ordered the Tandoori Chicken ($13.50 - chicken leg marinated with yogurt, ginger, more garlic baked in Tandoor oven) and the Navratna Curry ($12.25 - Mixed vegetables cooked with creamy onion tomato and cashew sauce). I can wholeheartedly recommend both dishes. The chicken in the tandoori dish was flavorful and juicy, with big hunks of chicken (good for 2-4 people to share). The star of the night was the Navratna Curry - creamy, flavorful, and rich, the dish is nirvana to eat and will have you licking the empty dish to be sure that you get every morsel.

In sum, Ekta Indian Cuisine was a joy to visit and I would happily come back again and again. Defintely mark this down as a must-visit in Bryn Mawr.
Fresh delicious bowls- great customer service! Refreshing drinks . Love it and can't wait to go back.

This supermarket is a great addition to the city. It's a literally giant modern supermarket. Upon entering the store you can find a nice Starbucks cafe. There's an escalator for human and escalator for the shopping cart. On the second floor there's a door to go to a nice size patio to enjoy beautiful weather and city view. Near the floral section you can find French macarons, cupcakes, and gelato. You pay it there. The macarons are pretty good but a bit too sweet for me. 

There are rows of handhelds scanners that links to your Giant card. You scan your groceries and pay what you bought. I haven't tried it but a friendly employee explained it to me.

If you don't have a Giant card you can apply at the customer service. It allows you to get special prices. Also at the customer service, you can find a map  of the store which is handy due to the size of the store.

There's kombucha station by the produce section. There are some local products such as varieties of peanut butter and almond butter. Once you pass the produce you will find sushi station where sushi are preapared on site, Salad works, prepared foods including varieties of uncooked flatbreads that can be made there. As you follow along on the left you will find Mission Taqueria. Their main restaurant is at 15 and Sansom. At the back area you will find varieties of beer on tap, in the fridge, and on the shelf. You can find soft pretzel to, perfect companion for the beer. There are seating areas and tv to watching sports. Also you can enjoy the view of river while you are enjoying your beer and or dessert, which are available next to the beer section. You don't feel like you're in a supermarket. 

What is special about this supermarket is that they carry a lot of local products: High Street Bread, Isgro's pastries, Claudio's pasta and cheese, DiBruno's cheese spread. Next to the deli station is the cannoli counter where you can pick the shell ( regular or chocolate covered), choose the fillings (regular, strawberry, blueberry) and put some toppings if you like. However I don't recommend the regular cannoli, it's bland. 

They have products that I've never seen them n other stores such as plantain flour, crepe mix. Most importantly, at the meat section they have dry-aged beef in a special glass display.

There's also a special section dedicated for plant-based food.

At the checkout, there are several self service and the regular ones. The register station is similar to the ones at Trader Joe's. No conveyor belt, small space to put your groceries so you have to unload them a bit at a time. Very environmentally friendly with paper bags.

In summary, this supermarket gives you a different kind of experience. I called it grocery shopping with a view.

Finally got the chance to stop by Cheu this past weekend.  Definitely one of those situations where you're super excited to find a new favorite spot, but know its so close to home that you're going to want to go 2-3 times a week.  We snagged a seat at a table but now regret that as the counter seems the way to go if you are lucky enough to get a spot.  Next time.  

There's a solid drink menu with cocktails, canned craft beer, sake, wine....definitely impressed with all the Sixpoint choices, even had Resin on special.  Kudos to the two old timers that decided to go for the last two...

My wife and I split as much food as possible to get a good sampling of the menu and we weren't disappointed with a single thing.  We started with fresh tomatoes with yuzu, ricotta, watermelon, and mint along with the charred corn with miso truffle, and black vinegar.  Both were incredible and very different.  Either dish would bring me back.  We then had a pork belly bun and a cheeseburger bun.  These were almost like perfect little (southern) biscuits....my god I could eat 20.  The BBQ pig tails with peach kimchi and seasame...finger-licking good.  Then we finished off splitting the brisket noodle bowl with matzo ball, kimchi, egg noodles, chili broth.  The homemade chili oil spiced this up nicely and I can't wait to have this in the winter.  

Man, soooo good. I love love love this place and can't wait to go back. I don't know if we ordered super well or if this place is really just that good.

Dewey's is such a solid place to get pizza. It's always great and I don't have a single negative thing to say!

Their wine list is great, as is the entire menu. I love their salads (not as a pizza replacement, of course. Are you crazy?) I love to switch up the pizzas I order, but sometimes end up going with a classic pepperoni. The Green Lantern, Killer Veggie, and Meatball pizzas are all winners. Dessert is always great, too, and is usually from another local spot.

I love the window where kids can watch the pizza being tossed as they wait for theirs to come out. It definitely helps keep them occupied.

Service at this location is always so friendly. We will be back sometime very soon!

I've ordered Insomnia Cookies twice now and although I'm not bowled over, I'm not completely turned off.

It's a great concept - you can order hot from the oven cookies that are delivered 24/7 to your door. Great for those hard core party nights when you have few munching choices and certainly shouldn't be behind the wheel, or if you're up all night studying/working and just need a snack break.

I've ordered from my office and brought the cookies home to my girlie, and she's a fan of the stuffed cookies. I've ordered 6 packs and chosen double chocolate - ho hum, chocolate chip - not bad, white chocolate macadamia nut - my favorite of the bunch, and birthday cake - not my favorite. I thought I'd show them some COVID love since the college students are gone right now and the area is relatively quiet compared to pre corona times.  My wait was much longer than I could've anticipated each time. I'm talking like an hour wait for a box of 6 cookies and a stuffed cookie. WHAT?!  

The cookies were definitely hot, but they don't taste homemade. More like store bought, pre-made dough. And I'm not taking Nestle toll-house dough either. I mean like generic. grocery store brand, refrigerator case dough.

The pluses:

Contactless delivery
Cookies are hot
Delivered 24/7
ugh. guilty pleasure - cheese fries and a cheese steak being delivered late night after drinking! friendly drivers dropping off the delivery and good pricing.

Well seriously, who doesn't love Remedy. It's the perfect place to go on a date or meet up with your girl friends for coffee chats that last hours. I've had some great drinks and food at this location and some even better heart to hearts with great friends. The staff are wonderful people, it's close to campus making it an ideal off campus, but still kinda close venue to get away and study. 

I find this location is quite busy so you may have to wait a little while to find a table. Over all though I would never pass on an opportunity to hit up remedy, it's pretty rad!

I have eaten here now a few times. I'd have to say I had a more difficult time coming up with a review than most restaurants because what they do well is very good but there are also some dishes that disappoint. 

Food: The noodle soup is very good. It is authentic and is like something served in Chicago, NYC, Seattle, etc. The broth is spicy with some sourness and sweetness, going best with ground pork. The laab is also pretty good. Nice and mint forward which is always nice! The thai basil (pad ka prao) is served with minced meat which is the authentic style, however, taste is a bit underwhelming. The chicken satay was pretty bad. The chicken seemed undercooked throughout, rubbery, and was just simply not quality. This is a shame because their peanut sauce is homemade and very tasty. Spring rolls also looked great in presentation but almost seemed store bought with a mushy veg mix inside. Overall very mixed, and a bit disappointed they don't incorporate other authentic dishes such as thai sausage, moo ping, Khao moo dang, Khao mun gai, etc. 

Drinks: very good. When they mention it is a cocktail lounge with thai food, this is a true statement. The cocktails are nuanced and complex, comparing close to other cocktail lounges in the area. 

Ambiance and service: both great, they did a fantastic job taking over the old black market and renovating it to their own.

Yes, I know I just recently reviewed this place.  But dang it, I cannot stay away, or say enough good stuff about Reno Coffee Company.  Too often the fancy espresso drinks get all the glory.  But you will find none of those here.  Instead, the owners and staff have taken the art of brewed coffee to a whole new level.

They start with great quality beans, which you can also purchase bags of for your own use, and from there craft individually tailored beverages for each customer.  They grind the beans for each cup to order, then brew with an ingenious set of pour-over contraptions built right into the countertop.  You can take the coffee straight up to enjoy the quality of the beans on their own, or elect for a few add-ons.  The Average Joe is the option to customize your brew with the additions of dairy, assorted types of sweeteners, whether you like it frothed, and finished with a dusting of spices.  Some may point out the smaller selection of customizable options here, but I would argue what they do have all help accentuate the coffee itself, without weighing down with too many other flavors.  Also on the menu are two signature drinks of their own.  One is the Morning Mojito, an iced coffee sweetened with agave and the addition of mint.  The other is the Bullet Proof, mixed with Ghee, MCT Oil and brown sugar, is a drink where you will for sure feel a real spring in your step after drinking.  Beyond the coffee, there is hot chocolate and tea available, along with a few bottled beverages in the fridge case, but no food options, which I actually view as a plus in a place like this.  Prices are definitely a bit higher than pretty much anyplace else for brewed coffee, with you often paying about a dollar or so more than other cafés.  However, I think this is well worth the price tag, as you are getting some serious quality packed into your cup, and if you think about it, they are no more pricey than those fancy espresso drinks.

The service here is absolutely fantastic, and exceptionally friendly.  The staff here really know their stuff, and make what is by far the fastest pour-over brew I have ever seen.  The shop itself is tiny, smaller than most offices, and tucked back in the alley that is Roff Street down by the river in the bottom of the Arlington Tower.  There is just room enough for the counter and a few seats by the window, but don't let that deter you, since this place is exceedingly charming, and there is a very lovely little garden patio just outside which I will eagerly be looking forward to sitting in when the nice weather returns.  The place is easy enough to find if you are walking around the neighborhood, but parking might be a bit tight.

All in all, this is for sure my favorite coffee shop in town these days.  I have been nothing but impressed, and I can't recommend them highly enough.  I will certainly be back, eager to snag perhaps the best Cup O' Joe in the city.  Cheers!

I've been to this location a few times over the past few years and I noticed on my most recent visit that quality of food and the location, itself, has gone downhill.  Not to mention, there isn't much value for the quality/quantity of food you get compared to similar restaurants in the area. 

The restaurant felt dingy and our booth was a bit sticky and grimy.  Service was pretty average and the Grilled Chicken on the Barbie I had was actually flavored very well.  I did substitute the mixed vegetables for 4 sprigs of asparagus, which resulted in an upcharge for some reason.  Also, for an entrée that included a breast of chicken and four asparagus, I don't know that $20 seems like a good deal.

I'm always looking for something new to try in Mount Airy, so when JJ suggested we go to Grain Exchange for brunch, I was all in. We easily found street parking about half a block away, and walked into a nearly empty bar (it was early). 

Although I appreciated the sign advertising $5 breakfast cocktails (!!!), I wasn't really in the mood to drink, so I ordered coffee instead. The coffee was light and received generous refills from our perky waitress (who paid us a lot of attention, possibly because we were her only table for a while). I ordered the fried waffles Benedict, which was an unexpected but welcome combo. One large waffle and two large pieces of fried chicken came with a side of syrup. On the other side of the plate, one perfectly poached egg coated in bearnaise sauce sat atop a small muffin-sized cup of hash brown. I loved this plate, mostly because it came with both of my favorite brunch items without being too large to handle.

This is a hidden gem for brunch in Mount Airy. In addition to the meat-eater's options, I was also impressed that a full half of the menu was devoted to vegetarian items. I'm looking forward to returning for another visit!

I should know better than to exit a drive-thru without thoroughly checking the order first. It was dinner hour and I guess I didn't want to hold up the line. Regardless, got home to find I had been given the wrong bowls, no salsa, no receipt, and no sign of the side I'd ordered. Obviously, they handed me the next guy's order, and I paid a 25% premium for what I received. Hope he enjoyed my food.
Nasty flavor. Very sloppy presentation. Empty on Thursday night. Never again. Best part was the serve staff.

The cheesesteaks here are the truth! I'm on a Facebook page about cheesesteaks and people time and again rave about Curly's so I knew we had to go. The cheesesteaks certainly didn't disappoint. They are gigantic and on a delicious seeded roll that maintains its structural integrity. The meat was perfect and I really enjoyed their cheese sauce. Top three cheesesteak experience for me. 
The wings were good but not my favorite. We got the Buffalo garlic parm sauce and thought it could probably have more flavor. The fries weren't our favorites. They serve seasoned fries that we just didn't care for but I'm sure lots of people love them. I definitely want to come back for some of that macaroni and cheese that looks amazing. 
The only thing besides the fries I wasn't crazy about was the 3% convenience fee to use a credit card. No other place does that for food that I know of. I can't imagine it makes a huge different in their bottom line, but it certainly leaves a bad taste for the customer.

If you have to self aggrandize yourself, then you really know you are in trouble. 
An example that comes to mind is when we were driving through South Carolina and stopped at a place that self titled themselves as having the best breakfast in the country. What we ended up getting was greenish steak and eggs and a crab omelet that sent the Girly into vomit mode. N'awlins Cafe & Spic is another example.
This is a place that should NEVER, ever serve food to people...ever. 
If you want gumbo that would make a geriatric go, "Hey! Where's the flavor?" then this might actually be a place you might want. 
And as someone else mentioned, they don't clean their shrimp too well.
All in all, you can pay less and get more and better all over the place. Keep walking.

My quest to eat at every Dot's location is now complete. If you're looking for a classic diner with good, reasonably priced, food find yourself any of Dot's five locations.

In case you were not aware, I am a ridiculous human being. A while back one of my co-workers suggested we get breakfast for lunch and that Dot's is where we should go. We went, and it was great. That is when we decided we should visit every location and see which is the best. 

This location is probably the largest Dot's and it is located across the street from Ochsner's main campus. It also appears to be the newest location. So it's new and across from one of the biggest hospitals in the area. That must mean it is jam packed right? Sort of.

We ended up here one day right around 11:00 a.m. While it was busy, it was not packed. As we finished our meal it started getting more crowded and had a wait. Moral of the story? Get there at 11:00.

Service was friendly, but not as personal as some other locations. I attributed this to how much larger and busier this location was. The food was classic breakfast fare. The eggs were cooked perfectly as were the meats. Dot's definitely knows how to make a solid breakfast.

So where does this rate for me in comparison to the other four Dot's? I'd say this is #3.

I went in on a Saturday .   I was pretty hungry and needed something quick. Turned out that wasn't gonna be possible.  A couple of guys were ahead of me and as tap rooms are a selection overload, it took a bit to get them served.  

I ordered up the Beer and Brat special and went to sit down.   The beer selection for the special was limited to germans so I got a tasty kolsch.  It went down quickly and I ordered up a flight of the more interesting sours and went back to my socially distanced table for one.  

The brat came out switftly and it went down just as quick.  I wasn't too fond of the bun...it basically disintegrated in my hand.  The tots were good and hit the spot.

The price point as shown in my receipt was a bit stiff for me.   And the space is pretty small.

I will keep an eye on their selection and maybe just grab some of the beers to go next time.  And then hit them up post covid for a regular and real experience.

The fried mac and cheese was great in theory and pretty decent in execution, but for some reason I detected an odd sour flavor in mine. I hope this was because they use sour cream or some sourish kind of cheese, and not because the milk they used had gone bad. 

The muffuletta was okay, but nowhere near the same ballpark as the ones I had in New Orleans. Maybe the Trainwreck should have been the way to go.

FINALLY found pho Cam Ly open again, everything was as good as ever.

No real explanation for the unexplained closure(s).

Food & service are GREAT, parking may be very difficult.

Still a FAVORITE !

Rick
Covid 19 update: they are open for takeout. They have been busy enough that they haven't been able to answer the phone sometimes which is cool

Boy did I miss the ol' Han Dynasty... once he left Exton to his parents it just wasn't the same. But here its hitting on all cylinders. Ordered up a mix of lamb cumin, pork belly, dry pot beef, etc. Nicely portioned and priced lunch dishes (think larger than an app, but smaller than a dinner plate). 
PS - Bartender did great on the caprahinna's after we taught him the ropes. 

if you're a fan of GOOD Chinese, put HD on the list. Damn I wish it were closer...

Basil Spice really takes on the presentation game. Especially in comparison to 99% of the other Thai restaurants in the area. So you get a wonderful presentation of delicious Thai food, set to as spicy as you would like it to be, and have a little room for the sticky rice dessert, which is simple, yet indulgent. I like the pad thai, of course, but definitely try the pad see iw. Noms.

If you like spicy, they can certainly make it spicy for you. Try it, if you can!

If you want huge portions, go to a buffet. Sometimes the "Wow" factor in American dining has people so wound up in huge portion sizes that they forget that food is something more than just filler for your stomach. It's about flavor and culture.

Tria Cafe is definitely another great Sunday lunch spot, especially with their Sunday school offers (i.e. day time happy hour). Service may be spotty, so be prepared to ask for the same thing for a few times

Must get: bruschetta (mushroom and goat cheese), mixed green salad, sunday Cheese and wine pairing
Meh: white bean spread, crab stuffed peppers, chocolate torte, bread pudding
Missed: sandwiches

I really loved our time at Tria Cafe, and with some minor gripes, I think everyone should visit this establishment at least one Sunday a year. For my yelp followers, I dislike brunch with its emphasis on different plays on egg and charging an exorbitant amount of money on it. Tria, at least on Sundays, only open at 12pm, making it easier to visit a popular brunch spot without fighting the usual crowd. My favorite dishes at Tria had to be the mushroom bruschetta and the cheese of the day (goat cheese for our visit). I think the blend of the mushroom and cheese worked so well for a toughened, flavored bruschetta and the cheese was served at a very generous portion. As for the other dishes, the crab stuffed peppers looked so great on paper, but was a definite hard pass as it just left me mouth feels: cold and wet - not at all what I  had imagined or liked.

The mixed green salad was probably my favorite 'large' dish option that was also easiest on the wallet. The pine nuts and dried figs definitely made each bite a true delight, just wished there was more greens.

As for the dessert, we had ordered the dessert very early on due to an earlier departure, but that request was completely forgotten and we had almost missed our after-lunch appointment. Also, the taste was below expectation, as the bread pudding tasted like a pre-made block of pound cake with unripe banana serving as garnish.

Overall, if you come here for the Sunday School Wine/cheese tasting, it is great! 

Food: 4 stars
Price: 3-4 stars
Service: 3 stars
Ambiance: 3 stars

We entered through the long bar and cafe, before entering you will pass through closely knit tables and planters on the sidewalk and a very narrow walk through. The bar was empty when we went for brunch, with plenty of bar stools and high topped tables lining the window.

We opted to sit in one of the dining rooms, which was more like an enclosed sun room and we welcomed the warmth shining in on us. Both dining rooms were filling up quickly and by the time we left the cafe and bar was doing a nice business.

Michael ordered a coke and I thought I would be cute and help myself to their amazing bloody mary bar! Be very careful of that London mix! Way too much London mix and not enough tomato juice and ye gads, for every sip I took I drank one half glass of water!

I was torn between the brisket hash with poached eggs or the fish and chips, but was quickly informed they were out of the hash. We started with the soup of the day which was a cream of potato soup, which I knew was either burned or old or both when I first saw it, as it was not a creamy white but a very distinct tan in color. I had one bite, hubs thought it was okay and decided to finish it.

A fresh fruit salad is served complimentary to each table which was a pleasant surprise. Michael ordered the eggs benedict which can also be ordered eggs Florentine. The eggs were perfectly cooked and the ham was nice and thick with a beautifully made hollandaise sauce. He had asked for them to add onions and peppers to his home fries and they were happy to oblige. He was thrilled with his order and raved over the home fries in particular. As a side he ordered bacon and their bacon is not wonderful, it was thin and very soft.

I loved their hand cut fries and you get enough to feed an army! Three pieces of fish topped my huge plate of fries, the breading was way too thick and not cooked on the inside, even though the fish was cooked perfectly throughout. I ate half of one piece and decided to concentrate on the fries, bringing the fish home to reheat in the oven until the breading was cooked. I like a good crunchy fish and this was certainly not the best I've ever had.

There is one restroom at the top of a very steep and very dirty flight of stairs, I don't think the carpets had been swept in quite some time, the restroom was clean.

I must compliment the staff, I have not witnessed such great teamwork in a long time. It did not matter if it was your section or your table, whoever was in the area cleared your plates as needed, refilled drinks, brought out food, the service was friendly and spot on!

One of my favorite restaurants in Nashville. Located across the street from Ascend, The Green Pheasant has a mix of classiness with a good modern vibe. A lot of Japanese inspired dishes and entrees that will make you head spin. Service was excellent!! 

Looking forward to many more visits

No. The bison burger was no good. First, charging for all different condiments doesn't make gourmet. Online prices were different from what I was told over the phone. Whoever picked up the picked up the phone was not professional.
Bison is very lean, so can be dry, but "medium" became a hard rock with no flavor. I would have added some mirim to tenderize. Chipotle sauce? Spicy and sour is not chipotle. 5 pieces of onion rings =$2. Unoriginal, badly cooked. Not coming back.

Cash only.  $10 for a heap pile of food.  This place is about quantity and not quality. 
Meatloaf is mostly bread.

Have been coming here since it open. The pasta and pizza has lost its way and now taste more like Store supermarket pizza and pasta than what I am used to. We ordered several pasta dishes and 2 pizza slices.  All of us which is a rarity were disappointed. The pesto pasta had pesto that look like it came out of a can and tasted as such. 
This is the second time. Can never come back again.

They didn't get nice to the chicken or the chicken sandwich for that matter.  Chick-fil-a did and that is why you should go down the street and get good food done quickly.  Two chickens sandwiches ordered.  Chicken was greasy and the bun was way too toasted.  Fries are battered therefore terrible.  Coleslaw was half full and swimming in dressing.  No reason honest here when you have Burger King across the street. McDonald's and the king of all fast food CHICK-FIL-A right down the street.  Not sure what all the hub bub is about?  People really settle for average food and claiming to be amazing.  Hopefully they close up shop soon and get a mom and pop eatery on that space.

Offensive.  Flavorless pre-made guacamole in a tiny bowl.  i've been robbed less at overpriced restaurants in manhattan.  what really got me was that it turned brown by the time we were down to the last chip (tops 10 minutes).  pampano, mama mexico, even dos caminos makes fresh guac at your table to order.  are you serious "So-Cheet"?  within the first 20 minutes of being at this restaurant, i can understand why they did an opentable spotlight.

The housemade chips aren't overly greasy and they're the perfect thickness, but is all that salt necessary?

The mushrooms in the tamale were tasty, but I thought the strong cheese didn't fit in, I could barely taste the pasilla chile gravy or house crema, so I can't say they added much.

Extremely dry, tough and tasteless short ribs--maybe this was the "lean kind" but it was really really dry/tough.  the golden potato gordita was great, but the roasted garden veggies were soggy, soaking in sauce, and just as uninteresting as the short ribs.

Completely dry and tasteless chicken (the special "pollo frito")--$25 for a half-chicken, are you serious?!  i made a more tender chicken breast on a fry pan the other night.  (entrees were $30 post-tax across the board--at those prices, I'd go to one of the amazing BYOs Philly offers)  24-hour brined does not make dry chicken!!!

The doughnuts were an incredible disappointment.  the chocolate sauce and whatever fruity sauce (they called it strawberry) they brought out seemed like they came out of a can and a film formed on the top of the chocolate almost instantly.  they should learn a thing or two from the stanton social.

I would give this place 2 stars but for the fact that the service was pretty terrible despite the very nice disposition of the waitress.  finished glasses and plates were not taken away unless we stacked them on top of each other and placed literally on the edge of the table.  the restaurant was hardly full so there was no excuse.  i specifically asked for no salt on my margarita, but surprise surprise, it came with salt.  my friend's first drink of the night never got cleared from the table--with 4 people at the table, are...you...for real?  i can appreciate paying happy hour prices for these drinks, but aside from the fresh ingredients like lemons, limes, mint, truly nothing to warrant the $10-15 cocktails (the cocktails go up in price from there).  the flights of tequila we tried were delicious but they could not make up for the fact that the food sucks.  sorry for the bluntness.
Still empty shelves. My Wife's Sister's Raley's doesn't have these problems. The Manager should spend less time writing insincere replies here and more time getting product to restock.

3.5 stars.

I went here with a group of Yelpers after a visit to Dock Street. It's somewhat dingy and dark at night. There are two sections, the main bar and another section with sit down booths and a karaoke machine. Fortunately, or unfortunately depending on your view of karaoke, it was karaoke night. Sort of like American Idol tryouts in real time, without the pompous British arsehole. There were actually some good singers, though by in large it was mostly juiced up partiers with an exaggerated opinion of their singing ability. The highlight was watching this guy dressed in Avril Lavigne arm bands singing and dancing without a care in the world. 

Drink prices were ridiculously cheap. I think for 4 people we got two gin an tonics an two beers for under 20 bucks. The waitress was attentive and fast.

I have to admit this wasn't a place I would have visited if friends hadn't prodded me into going. I'm glad I did.

Nice little piano bar/restaurant. They were unable to seat us for dinner, so we just posted up at the bar (where you can order food anyway) and eventually got enough seats for our group.

Their menu selection is a step up from what I would've expected. A lot of Asian-inspired dishes, and some nicer stuff. I got the duck confit crostini, and the grilled lamb. It was all pretty good. My friend got the Brussels sprouts that came topped with crushed ramen, which was funny.

Heard some fun piano music, ate some food, had a bunch of whiskey (they have a great selection). All in all, a great night.

Ahhh dive bars. 
Oh how I love thee. 
Danny's is just one of many, but most definitely a favorite. 
Cannot lie, we are there frequently enough to be known not only by name but by drink of choice that is nicely poured (and priced) as well. 
Juke box has a great selection and clientele is almost as varied as the juke box. Back patio makes for great conversation and friend making. No seriously, I have made 2 life-long friends cause of this place. 
Bathrooms are...well, dive bar bathrooms so don't expect much. Just don't touch more than you need to, do your thing and get outta there.

Everything here is a hit. I've personally had the "Buffy the puffy" as well as their guacamole..... Both fantastic. 

This is my favorite restaurant in the Channelside area. You really cant go wrong with the food and drinks. I highly recommend their frozen margarita. The location and the ambiance of this restaurant is great. You can sit and eat outside with downtown views or sit inside where they have so many tvs-- its a great place to watch the games.

I'm really struggling with this review. I've eaten at numerous Dickey's all around. The location in Love Field airport is my favorite. Airport food shouldn't be better than my local joint. 

The staff tonight was super friendly. Just two guys working and there was only 1 takeout guest The whole time we were dining, which is odd at peak dining hours. 

Ordered the deal of the day- pork sandwich (skipped the bread) , 2 sides, and big yellow cup for $9.99

The pork was good but cold by the time we got to the table. Slaw was good. I tried something new and tried the fried okra. It was made to order and pretty good. 

The food was good, but just not great. I'd probably opt for another bbq joint next time I'm craving bbq.

After several recommendations from locals, I decided to give Ichiban a try. The nigiri's were very good, nice sake, japanese scallop. The scallop was maybe the best thing I had all night. Also had uni nigiri which was ok...feel like it could have been fresher...didn't have that same ocean brinyness.  Also got the caterpillar role which was pretty tasty.

I ordered kama as well and it took probably 45 minutes to come out for some reason. And the hamachi didn't taste as fresh or maybe because it was overcooked. I wondered if that was to mask the freshness? It was just ok at best which was unfortunate, because a well cooked kama can melt in your mouth and this felt like eating canned tuna.

Lastly, price was also ok...a bit pricey for the locale being a neighborhood restaurant and the quality being a bit hit or miss.

If you are looking for something different to eat in NE philly, this is your place! The blue duck is a delicious and cute little restaurant. I have been here multiple times and had a few burgers and a few of the mac and cheese entrees and I was never disappointed. I have yet to try it for lunch. The service is top notch too. I hope to get back here soon!

Finally made it to three carrots today for lunch and im glad I did. We are all trying to lie about our new years diet but with this restaurant a block away from my work, I wont have to lie. At this funky little bistro you dont realize your eating healthy vegetarian food. I havent had anything i didnt like and the meals and drinks are fairly proced. Im going back and you should to.

Decided to try out a breakfast spot near my brother's home and this was an unexpected surprise.

Atmosphere - Cute kitchen themed breakfast-brunch spot. They have all the fresh pastries lined up on racks for display between the restaurant and the kitchen. The bar area is laid out in a way to allow for lots of patrons to sit and eat. I found the whole decor to be very welcoming. 

Service - We were promptly seated at the bar where we had exceptional service from the bartender. We asked about the best Dissents that were available and were given a strong recommendation for the Churro-style. Our drinks were served quickly and were greeted throughout the meal asking if there was anything eel we needed. 

Bloody Mary - Not overly spicy, but enough tang to keep it sweet and bold. The toppings of cheese, salami, pickle, and olive were a great balance to the drink. I loved the small beer back that came with the order. A solid choice for a morning beverage ($5)

Churro Dossant - Recommended by the bartender as it was his, "favorite flavor of dossant they make". I have had many dossants, doughssants, or cronuts, but have found that many are greasy or fall apart once you bite into them. This one did not! It was a wonderfully light and fluffy pastry with a thick layer of cream cheese frosting that paired so well with the cinnamon and sugar. ($4)

Biscuits & Gravy - Cheddar biscuit, sausage, and duck fat gravy that sounds as good as it tasted! I could have used a little more gravy, but the flavors in every bite made it so wonderful! I added two eggs over easy for $2. ($6)

I was very impressed by the place overall and am hoping to go back and try more pastries and breakfast before i leave! Well done guys!

A lively bar with lively food with a bit of a Mexican twist.

We stopped in here for a great drink made from orange juice and rum and enjoyed their famous Nashville hot fried chicken taco.  The taco had huge chunks of fried white meat drenched in a spicy cayenne pepper sauce with a dab of cooling coleslaw on top and served on a street-taco-sized flour tortilla.  The sweet cool drink was the perfect pairing to douse the fireworks from the hot chicken.

Other items on the menu also looked good and even at lunch there was live music which was really nice.  I'd definitely stop back and try them again.

Upgrading this to 5 stars. I've now visited this place at least a half a dozen times and the food and service is just consistently great.  This evening I had a salmon Florentine for dinner and it was fantastic. The draft beer selections have become more varied and interesting and the place is always clean and the atmosphere really enjoyable (get a seat near the fireplace this time of year).
Wow.  How do you screw up a finner salad.  Managers response to wilted brown lettuce.   "Thats how it all is" 46.00 for two sad dinners.  We could have had a good meal for that.  This location sucks.

Great service overall the staff was friendly and punctual, Steven is truly an amazing server. He took his time explaining the menu, his infectious smile and sociable personality makes him a great asset. I ordered the Blackened fish Étouffée. It was Delicious!!! Just enough seasoning and full of great flavor... Highly recommend!

First off, I've had Korean BBQ elsewhere in various cities because I absolutely love this type of food. I am not going to hold New Orleans to those standards due to its inexperience, but I couldn't help but score this low!

Appearance:
When you walk in, it looks really pleasing to the eyes due to its modern look. There are various TVs hanging on the columns and walls as well as a soft music background playing for the restaurant. It is very spacious and the lighting has a blue hue to it which really had showed on the white furniture. 

Food: 
First off, my party of 8 had ordered two servings Combo #4 which had various meats. The portions were suffice, but lacked whatever flavor it was supposed to have. It had tasted as though it was quickly dipped into the marinade and given to us. We had also ordered the seafood pancake, but it was a bit lackluster due to its lack of flavor and texture. The sauce given with it did not exemplify its qualities as well. We had also ordered the chicken wings, but I had felt that I was paying more for flavored bones because the wings were so stingy on meat. 

Service:
It was not great, but it wasn't TOO terrible. When we got our orders in, they did turn on our burners on until after the meat had already arrived at the table. When they did turn on the burner, they had only turned on one and didn't show nor tell us how to turn the other one on. I had to have one of my party members ask one of the servers passing by to help turn on the other burner. We also did not have any of the sauces for the meats given to us, so when we had asked for the sauces - they only gave one serving for the eight of us to share(?). 

Overall:
This place is new and I understand that, but maybe actually open the place when you have a full menu instead of having a menu with sheets of paper 'hiding' what isn't available and stock up on drink options. Food and service needs to be touched up on as well. This would be more acceptable if it were still in a soft opening phase, but not to be in its full functioning(?) phase.

Ok. We did come for the beer. But... we left with so much more. Our server, Alexis (please forgive us if the spelling is wrong), was everything we love in not just a service oriented person but a person in general. She was intuitive, super funny, VERY knowledgeable and above all, kind. Our food was amazing!!! Please try the chili! A sweet Smokey flavor with zing! The Buffalo chicken Cobb was fantastic!!! We think Bern's has some competition with the French Onion soup TBBC served. It was amazing!!! The burger was super as well!!! We can't say enough about the patience the staff had with us given all of our questions. Thank you for such a wonderful experience! Please let Alexis know she is blessed with a beautiful spirit and a special gift to serve!
The food was great and portion size was excellent. Decor was a little dated, but hey it's N'awlins. Good prices and excellent service. Would recommend giving them a geaux.
